macro_segments.title: Introduction to Finding Debt Documents (Shtar Chov) and the Question of Return

micro_segments.title: Setting the Context: Finding a Debt Document and the Basic Mishnah Rule
The Mishnah's Distinction: Liens Present versus Absent
Rabbi Meir's View: With Liens Do Not Return; Without Liens Return
The Sages' View: Never Return Regardless of Liens

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Opening Question: What is Shimeon's Claim in the Mishnah?

micro_segments.title: Interpreting 'Bemai Askinan': Is Shimeon Admitting or Denying the Loan?
The Gemara's First Answer: Shimeon Admits He Owes, But We Fear Predated Liens
Why Predated Liens Are Problematic: The Concern for Purchasers (Lakochot)

macro_segments.title: The Document's Suspicious Status: Why a Found Shtar is No Longer Valid Testimony

micro_segments.title: The Challenge: If No One Should Write Undated Documents, Why Suspect This One?
Rashi's Explanation: Carelessness with Lost Documents Suggests Dishonesty
The Lamudic Explanation: A Found Document Ceases to Be Valid Testimony

macro_segments.title: Writing Debt Documents Before Borrowing Money Occurs: The Kinyan Debate

micro_segments.title: The Question: Can Witnesses Sign Before the Money is Actually Lent?
Rav Assi's Answer: Only Shtar Hakna'ah (Document with Kinyan) Can Be Predated
Shtar Hakna'ah Explained: Granting Liens Today Even If Money Borrowed Later

macro_segments.title: Applying the Kinyan Rule Back to the Mishnah: Why We Still Cannot Return the Document

micro_segments.title: If the Document Has Kinyan, Why Not Return It to the Creditor?
If Without Kinyan, the Concern About Predating Should Never Arise
The Resolution: A Found Document May Represent Protocol Violation by Witnesses

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Alternative: Every Signature Creates Liens Retroactively (Edim Bechasimtan)

micro_segments.title: Abaye's Principle: Signatures Create Liens Effective as of the Signing Date
How Abaye's View Relates to Shtar Hakna'ah and Other Documents
The Gemara's Challenge: If Every Signature Works, Why Fear Pre-Dating?

macro_segments.title: Abaye and Conditional Effectiveness: Liens Take Effect Only When Document is Delivered

micro_segments.title: Clarifying Edim Bachasiman: Effectiveness Depends on Delivery of the Document
Comparison to Shtar Hakna'ah: When Delivery May Not Be Required
Retroactive Application: Liens Exist from Signing Date When Document is Eventually Delivered

macro_segments.title: Can We Return Documents Without Liens When Both Parties Agree? (Abaye's Problem)

micro_segments.title: If Both Parties Admit the Loan, and Liens Are Effective from Signing, Why Not Return?
Abaye's Alternative Explanation: Concern for Fraud and Schemes (Pirayon U'Knunya)
The Scheme Explained: Paid-Up Debt Used to Wrongly Seize Purchasers' Property

macro_segments.title: Shmuel's Rejection of Fraud Concerns and the Shift to Debtor Protection

micro_segments.title: Shmuel Does Not Fear Schemes or Fraud (Lo Chaishinan L'Pirayon U'Knunya)
The Consequence: Shmuel Must Reinterpret the Mishnah as Debtor Denial Case
When Debtor Denies: Document Cannot Be Returned Regardless of Liens

macro_segments.title: The Mishnah's Debate: With Liens vs. Without Liens When Debtor Denies

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Meir: Without Liens, Document is Worthless and Can Be Returned
Why Return Worthless Document? Only as a Bottle Stopper (Tzor Pi Tzluchiso)

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar and Rabbi Yochanan: Two Readings of the Mishnah's Scenario

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar: Mishnah Debates the Debtor Denial Case (Ein Chayav Modeh)

macro_segments.title: The Mishnah's Debate: With Liens vs. Without Liens When Debtor Denies (continued)

micro_segments.title: The Rabbis (Chachamim): Even Without Written Liens, Liens Are Presumed

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar and Rabbi Yochanan: Two Readings of the Mishnah's Scenario (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan: Mishnah Debates When Debtor Admits (Chayav Modeh)
The Difference: Protecting Creditor versus Protecting Purchasers

macro_segments.title: Baraisa Evidence: Explicit Proof that the Mishnah Assumes Debtor Admission

micro_segments.title: The Baraisa: Even When Both Parties Admit, Do Not Return If Liens Exist
Protecting Purchasers: The Concern for Schemes (Knunya) Applies Even With Admission
The Debate Limited to Implicit Liens: With Liens Written, No One Returns

macro_segments.title: How the Baraisa Contradicts Rabbi Elazar: The Document is Not Worthless

micro_segments.title: The Baraisa Shows: Without Written Liens, Document Still Collects from Non-Encumbered Property
Therefore Rabbi Meir's 'Worthless' Claim is Wrong; The Real Reason for Non-Return is Liens
And the Baraisa Confirms We Fear Schemes: Concern for Purchased Property (Lakochot)

macro_segments.title: Synthesis: The Unified Explanation of the Mishnah According to Rabbi Yochanan

micro_segments.title: The Core Issue: Presence or Absence of Liens (Not Debtor Admission)
The Concern: Protecting Purchasers Against Schemes (Pirayon U'Knunya)
The Debate: Do Liens Exist Implicitly Even Without Written Specification?
The Conclusion: Whenever Liens Exist, Never Return to Protect Purchasers

topical_tags.term: Nissan (First Month, New Year for Documents)
Edim Bechasimtan Zachin Lo (Signatures Create Rights)
